risk_if_not_implemented: Without Out-of-Band Channels, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Telecommunications Services Availability
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to reduce the likelihood of a single point of failure with primary telecommunications services.
compensating_control_1.justification: Telecommunications Services Availability (BCD-10) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Out-of-Band Channels (NET-11) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Technology applicability. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Remote Access
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to define, control and review organization-approved, secure remote access methods.
compensating_control_2.justification: Remote Access (NET-14) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Out-of-Band Channels (NET-11) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
